CVX.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 2026 earnings imminent (~April 24-May 1, 1-2 trading days). Net news sentiment: mixed-to-negative. The US-Iran ceasefire hit CVX -5.3% on April 8, and the company pre-disclosed $2.7-3.7B in Q1 earnings impact from Middle East commodity volatility. Partially offset by Wells Fargo raising PT to $222, Leviathan gas field production restoration, and Gulf of Mexico oil discovery. Net news flow: negative-leaning. Insider activity: net selling — Pate sold $8.6M in CVX shares with no offsetting buys; bearish lean. Technical setup quality: daily chart is not constructive heading into earnings. Price ($188.36) is below EMA20, EMA21, and EMA50. Daily MACD histogram is negative (-0.53), RSI-21 at 31 (oversold but in downtrend context). OBV is in a downtrend and diverging from price — a bearish distribution signal. ADX at 32 (strong trend) with -DI (28.59) > +DI (21.56) confirms bearish momentum. The 21-day ROC is -10.79%, reflecting the sharp sell-off. Hourly picture shows a strong intraday bounce (price at $192.21, above all hourly MAs, hourly RSI 70.25 overbought with bearish divergence) — this looks like a short-covering rally into earnings, not accumulation. The catalyst exists but the setup is poor: the known $2.7-3.7B headwind is an announced negative catalyst, insider is selling, and the daily chart is in a bearish structure. Per strategy rules, a catalyst with a poor technical setup going in = neutral. Not entering into a known-negative earnings print with a deteriorating chart.

WMT.reasoning: Catalyst window: Q1 FY2027 earnings on May 21, 2026 — approximately 15 trading days away. Slightly outside the preferred 10-day window but close enough to begin positioning given the constructive setup. The tariff resolution/escalation narrative is also an ongoing binary catalyst that can move the stock at any time. Net news sentiment: positive. Guggenheim raised PT to $137 (from $120) maintaining Buy. Digital sales +24% with first-ever profitable e-commerce quarter globally. US comps +4.5%, Sam's Club +6.7% ex-fuel. Consumer sentiment at record low (47.6 UMich) structurally benefits WMT's value positioning. Private label Great Value redesign (~10,000 items) supports margin expansion. Stock up 12-14% YTD — defensive flows intact. Negatives: Amazon surpassed WMT in sales; OpenAI partnership terminated; tariff exposure with 80% China-based suppliers; HSBC downgrade to Hold in February. Insider activity: net selling — Walton family selling is large ($4.6B) and recurring/structural, representing founders treating current levels as distribution. No insider buys. This is a headwind but not an acute near-term catalyst. Technical setup quality: constructive on the daily. EMA-aligned uptrend flag is TRUE — all EMAs (5/10/20/21/50/200) are in proper upward alignment. Price is above EMA20, EMA21, EMA50, EMA200. Daily MACD histogram positive (+0.1456). Daily OBV trending up, not diverging. The stock has pulled back from a $132.46 recent high to $127.59, which lands near key support: the $126.93 daily pivot (30 tests) and the hourly $127.33 support (25 tests). The pullback is healthy (z-score 0.83, not extended). Hourly RSI at 39.2 (approaching oversold, constructive for entry). The pullback to multi-tested support with an intact uptrend is a textbook pre-catalyst entry. Risk: Walton selling and tariff uncertainty could cap the upside. Target set at $134.24 (Fib ext 1.272 from the recent swing) near analyst consensus of $136.
